title: SIZES_OF_COLUMNS_DOESNT_MATCH with empty tuples
description:
### Describe the bug
Empty tuples again :/ Seems to happen on the current master only. cc @KochetovNicolai I think it's related to https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/pull/90309
### How to reproduce
Fiddle is giving a generic error. Run:
```sql
SET limit = 4;
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Tuple(), c1 Array(Int), c2 Int) ENGINE = AggregatingMergeTree() ORDER BY (c2);
INSERT INTO TABLE t0 (c0, c1, c2) VALUES ((), [1], 1), ((), [], 2), ((), [2], 3);
SELECT c0 FROM t0 FINAL ORDER BY c1; --SIZES_OF_COLUMNS_DOESNT_MATCH error
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:
```
<Error> executeQuery: Code: 9. DB::Exception: Size of permutation (3) is less than required (4). (SIZES_OF_COLUMNS_DOESNT_MATCH) (version 25.12.1.615 (official build)) (from 127.0.0.1:41560) (query 1, line 1) (in query: SELECT c0 FROM t0 FINAL ORDER BY c1;), Stack trace (when copying this message, always include the lines below):
0. contrib/llvm-project/libcxx/include/__exception/exception.h:113: Poco::Exception::Exception(String const&, int) @ 0x0000000025f29232
1. src/Common/Exception.cpp:131: DB::Exception::Exception(DB::Exception::MessageMasked&&, int, bool) @ 0x0000000015ca0a29
2. src/Common/Exception.h:123: DB::Exception::Exception(String&&, int, String, bool) @ 0x000000000d5dae94
3. src/Common/Exception.h:58: DB::Exception::Exception(PreformattedMessage&&, int) @ 0x000000000d5daa13
4. src/Common/Exception.h:141: DB::Exception::Exception<unsigned long, unsigned long&>(int, FormatStringHelperImpl<std::type_identity<unsigned long>::type, std::type_identity<unsigned long&>::type>, unsigned long&&, unsigned long&) @ 0x000000000eaaf7c6
5. src/Columns/ColumnTuple.cpp:498: DB::ColumnTuple::permute(DB::PODArray<unsigned long, 4096ul, Allocator<false, false>, 63ul, 64ul> const&, unsigned long) const @ 0x000000001e8431e8
6. src/Interpreters/sortBlock.cpp:368: DB::sortBlock(DB::Block&, DB::SortDescription const&, unsigned long, DB::IColumn::PermutationSortStability) @ 0x000000001c35fe13
7. src/Processors/Transforms/PartialSortingTransform.cpp:140: DB::PartialSortingTransform::transform(DB::Chunk&) @ 0x000000001fedc7e7
8. src/Processors/ISimpleTransform.h:33: DB::ISimpleTransform::transform(DB::Chunk&, DB::Chunk&) @ 0x0000000018592913
9. src/Processors/ISimpleTransform.cpp:98: DB::ISimpleTransform::work() @ 0x000000001fbcc38a
10. src/Processors/Executors/ExecutionThreadContext.cpp:53: DB::ExecutionThreadContext::executeTask() @ 0x000000001fbeaa30
11. src/Processors/Executors/PipelineExecutor.cpp:351: DB::PipelineExecutor::executeStepImpl(unsigned long, DB::IAcquiredSlot*, std::atomic<bool>*) @ 0x000000001fbdcc78
12. src/Processors/Executors/PipelineExecutor.cpp:279: DB::PipelineExecutor::executeSingleThread(unsigned long, DB::IAcquiredSlot*) @ 0x000000001fbdd2ac
13. src/Processors/Executors/PipelineExecutor.cpp:602: DB::PipelineExecutor::executeImpl(unsigned long, bool) @ 0x000000001fbdbc21
14. src/Processors/Executors/PipelineExecutor.cpp:136: DB::PipelineExecutor::execute(unsigned long, bool) @ 0x000000001fbdb2fe
15. src/Processors/Executors/PullingAsyncPipelineExecutor.cpp:76: void std::__function::__policy_func<void ()>::__call_func[abi:se210105]<ThreadFromGlobalPoolImpl<true, true>::ThreadFromGlobalPoolImpl<DB::PullingAsyncPipelineExecutor::pull(DB::Chunk&, unsigned long)::$_0>(DB::PullingAsyncPipelineExecutor::pull(DB::Chunk&, unsigned long)::$_0&&)::'lambda'()>(std::__function::__policy_storage const*) @ 0x000000001fbf3068
16. contrib/llvm-project/libcxx/include/__functional/function.h:508: ? @ 0x0000000015de9a83
17. contrib/llvm-project/libcxx/include/__type_traits/invoke.h:0: void* std::__thread_proxy[abi:se210105]<std::tuple<std::unique_ptr<std::__thread_struct, std::default_delete<std::__thread_struct>>, void (ThreadPoolImpl<std::thread>::ThreadFromThreadPool::*)(), ThreadPoolImpl<std::thread>::ThreadFromThreadPool*>>(void*) @ 0x0000000015df060e
18. ? @ 0x00000000000a3d64
19. __clone3 @ 0x00000000001373bc
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (c0 RECORD (c0 INT, c1 STRING), c1 ARRAY(INT), c2 INT) USING MERGE;
INSERT INTO t1 (c0, c1, c2) VALUES (ROW(NULL, NULL), ARRAY[1, 2], NULL), (ROW(NULL, NULL), ARRAY[], 3), (ROW(NULL, NULL), ARRAY[4], 5);
CREATE TABLE t2 AS SELECT * FROM t1;
INSERT INTO t2 (c0, c1, c2) SELECT c0, c1, c2 FROM t1 WHERE c2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT c0, COUNT(*) FROM t2 GROUP BY c0 ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC;
key_bug_features: Empty tuples in AggregatingMergeTree table creation
